reviews.author: Gustavo A. Pardo III
reviews.text: Very cute area great night life lots of diversity due to it being a focal point for Ha Giang loop. Great restaurants and drink spots local goods! Beautiful both day and night.

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: A unique night market, imbued with the culture of the northern mountainous region. There are many activities on weekends and at festivals. In the morning, can walk around this area to have a look at the ancient houses.

reviews.author: Clive Shilling
reviews.text: A nice area to walk around, shops, bars, cafe, market square. Go at night too, very pretty all lit up.

reviews.author: Edwin Sim
reviews.text: Nice ancient city with the Hmong tribe. Ambience is great, air is cool and fresh. Food & drinks everywhere at reasonable prices.

reviews.author: Hillary McCrumb
reviews.text: What a great town!! Tucked in the mountains this was the perfect way to immerse yourself in Northern Vietnam culture and relax after being on the Ha Giang Loop. And we were fortunate enough to see a festival that night celebrating all the ethnic groups of Vietnam.

reviews.author: ANH KHOA NGUYEN
reviews.text: Dong Van Ancient Street truly comes alive during holidays and festivals. The entire area is bustling with energy, offering plenty of food stalls serving delicious local specialties. For the best experience, visit at night when the atmosphere is at its most vibrant. You can join traditional games like throwing cotton ball game (10k vnd/ 3 times), dance with local people, enjoy a bonfire, and indulge in street food.

reviews.author: DANWAY HOLDINGS
reviews.text: Dong Van Old town at Tuyên Quang Vietnam. H'Mong minorities sunday morning market gathering. They are selling the home made foods where you may find local foods, mostly veggies from their own farms...the activities where they can meet up with friends and shopping for foods. Recommend to visit.

reviews.author: Chloe
reviews.text: Dong Van Old Quarter at night has a magical, sparkling beauty and many unique cultural activities for tourists. Here are some typical activities you can refer to: • Every evening, in the center of the old quarter, there are often cultural performances imbued with the ethnic cultural identity of the Mong, Lo Lo, Pu Peo... such as folk dancing, playing the flute, carrying lanterns... • Visitors can interact and join in the dances with ethnic boys and girls. • Restaurants in the old quarter become bustling at night, visitors can enjoy specialties of the highlands such as thang co, men men, black chicken hotpot... • Dong Van Old Quarter at night is lit up by brilliant lanterns, creating a romantic and magical space. • Visitors can walk around, admire the ancient houses, learn about the architecture and history of the quarter. • On weekends, coffee shops in the old town often organize cultural performances, visitors can sip coffee, enjoy music and admire the scenery. • Coffee shops with the ancient character of the mountainous region are an extremely suitable stop for visitors. • In the old town, there are many souvenir shops, visitors can buy unique gifts with strong local cultural identity.

reviews.author: Steve Ozark
reviews.text: A great walkabout and probably an great place to stay some nights.

reviews.author: Paul
reviews.text: It was our second stop on our 3 day Ha Giang tour. They provieded us with a cozy room, a mix of different dishes to eat for dinner and karaoke plus happy water in the evening. It was a good place to stay.

reviews.author: Trí Đức
reviews.text: Unique place with old buildings, very recommend to visit

reviews.author: Chuong Nguyen
reviews.text: Great little town for tourists. It's an old town with a good mix of traditional culture and many modern coffee and restaurants. Lovely mountains in the background really add to the charm of the village.

reviews.author: Jessica
reviews.text: OMG, this place is enchanting—misty, lantern-lit streets blend colonial charm with Hmong vibrancy. Hit the Sunday market for jewelry and street food. Stroll at dusk or sip local coffee. Booked a Joytime walking tour—witty guide, festival buzz, so lucky! A Ha Giang gem!

reviews.author: Hảo
reviews.text: Let me tell you about Dong Van Ancient Street. It wasn't quite what I pictured, but that wasn't a bad thing. I'd seen photos online of these perfectly preserved streetscapes, but the reality is a little more rugged. The houses are beautiful, don't get me wrong. These old stone structures with faded paint and mossy roofs lining a narrow street have a real charm, especially with the dramatic mountains as a backdrop. But it's clear they've lived a life. The whole place felt a touch worn around the edges, which for me added to the atmosphere. It's not a museum – it's a living, breathing town. Local women set up stalls overflowing with colorful fruits and vegetables, chatting as they wait for customers. Motorbikes zip past, their drivers calling out greetings. It can be a little chaotic, but that's part of the fun. Sure, it might not be everyone's cup of tea. If you're looking for polished perfection, this might not be it. But for me, Dong Van Ancient Street was a chance to see a glimpse of a different way of life, steeped in history and authenticity.

reviews.author: Luan Tran
reviews.text: Dong Van's old town is a vibrant representation of traditional culture, featuring multi-colored buildings that have stood for centuries. Influenced by French architecture from the 1880s, the town boasts important highlights, including the well-preserved Dong Van Market from the 1920s. Nestled among rocky mountains, the town is characterized by its 40 ancient rooftops and houses, some of which are nearly 200 years old.

reviews.author: Quang Do
reviews.text: Great town to visit in Ha Giang. From here you can easily visit Lung Cu Flag, Vuong family or Nho Que river. Will come back in the winter.

reviews.author: Alice Nguyen
reviews.text: Should visit here on Sunday morning for the local market festival - it is operated every weekend. I arrived Dong Van town on Sunday afternoon so I missed the opportunity 😭😭 Will come back there 2nd time. Anyway the price of souvenirs in Dong Van ancient market is cheap. I bought 4 shawls and some unique cakes for my friends and I feel good with the price 😁😁There has some mini bars with good music in this area, I didn't have enough time to enjoy but it looks nice. We can also go for the local massage - spa named Như Ý with good price and service ~

reviews.author: Marnix Van Der Meulen
reviews.text: Pretty town on the ha giang loop with. we came here by bus to walk the sky trail. Worth a visit even if you are not going by moterbike for the loop. Bus from ha giang to Dong Van is about 150.000 (5 euro/usd) takes about 6 hours depending your "Mario kart" driver. Easy to book at your acc. in ha giang. Bus back is near old market square at the main street. (We took the one at 10 am

reviews.author: Haris Rashid
reviews.text: Nice bars, nice restaurants, and good people. If you want to meet internationals, this place is a must in the evening. Not so much during Dec, but still great vibe and great food.

reviews.author: Hà Ngọc Dũng
reviews.text: It' great. You can by many kind of gift. Food, coffe is good. And At night you can danc around fire. It's so fun

reviews.author: Tan Minh Nguyen
reviews.text: Ancient town but has been remodel with a lot of food choices, drinks and souvenir shop. Great places for walking around when you staying at Dong Van.

reviews.author: Andrew Thurlow
reviews.text: Great strip for food, beer or coffee before or after the ride

reviews.author: Nguyetluatsu Nguyen
reviews.text: The wonderful place to find historical vietnamese culture

reviews.author: Chuyền Tony
reviews.text: Just so nice to walk though this old town after dinner it was beautiful area at the central of Đồng Văn town

reviews.author: Rajendrasinh Rajput
reviews.text: Very good place with lots of fun and activities. Food is very good here people are very friendly

reviews.author: Vũ Thị Kim Chi
reviews.text: Beautiful town. So many streets foood on the town. You guys can try rice cake with soup. Super good

reviews.author: Tran Anh
reviews.text: Old Town with small maket, quite and fresh air. You should sleep 1 night before go to Meo Vac.

reviews.author: Yeo's Say
reviews.text: Nice bit of civilization after tonnes of hillside views. Do try their tofu n rice ball (Tang Yuen) desserts. Still can't get my egg coffee😁

reviews.author: Sergio Marani
reviews.text: A charming glimpse into the past! During my Hà Giang Loop, I explored Đồng Văn Ancient Street, a beautifully preserved area with old H’mông-style houses, lanterns, and cozy cafés. The mix of local culture and colonial architecture creates a unique atmosphere, perfect for a quiet stroll. Morning markets here are lively, offering delicious street food and traditional crafts. A must-visit!

reviews.author: Martin Cavina
reviews.text: Great place for tourists. You have food, bars, coffe etc...
